turns.user_message: Someone I know casually mentioned that I seem a little scattered lately, and honestly it used to just bounce right off me. Now I keep replaying that exact moment in my head over and over again. I find myself checking every little thing I do just to make sure I am not making a mistake. It feels like my chest gets tight whenever I think about it, even though I know it was just a passing remark. I just wish I could stop overanalyzing every single word I say to people.

That same offhand comment keeps echoing in my mind no matter how hard I try to push it away. I catch myself freezing up before I speak because I am terrified of sounding incompetent or careless. It has turned into this constant background noise that drains my energy by the end of the day. I keep running through imaginary conversations where I try to prove that I am actually completely fine. The more I try to ignore it, the heavier my breathing gets and the harder it is to focus on anything else.

I am completely trapped in a loop of second-guessing myself because that one little remark has taken over my thoughts. Every minor hesitation now feels like a glaring flaw that everyone else is quietly noticing. My heart races constantly and my hands actually shake when I have to respond to simple questions. I feel like I am walking on thin ice all day long, waiting for the next moment I might mess up. It is exhausting trying to keep everything perfectly controlled when my own mind keeps tripping me up.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This used to not bother the speaker.
